story.tasks: - Task 1: Prisma migrations klasörünü oluştur ve .gitignore'ı yapılandır (AC: 1) - Task 2: İlk migration oluştur - init migration ile core tables (AC: 2, 4) - Task 3: package.json scripts ekle - migration commands (AC: 3) - Task 4: MongoDB için migration skip logic (AC: 5) - Task 5: README migration workflow dokümantasyonu (AC: 6) - Task 6: Integration test ve validation (AC: All)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: AC-1.5.1: `prisma/migrations/` klasörü oluşturulmuş (gitignore'dan exclude) AC-1.5.2: İlk migration oluşturulmuş (`init` migration - tüm core tables SQL) AC-1.5.3: Migration commands `package.json`'a eklendi (prisma:generate, prisma:migrate, prisma:deploy) AC-1.5.4: Migration başarıyla çalışıyor (PostgreSQL database'de tüm tablolar oluşuyor) AC-1.5.5: MongoDB için migration skip ediliyor (schema.prisma MongoDB ise migrate command hata vermiyor veya warning veriyor) AC-1.5.6: README'de migration workflow açıklanmış (adımlar documented)

docs.doc: Complete acceptance criteria specifications for migration system. Defines migration workflow, commands, PostgreSQL-specific implementation, MongoDB skip logic, and documentation requirements.

Complete database schema definition for all core entities: User, RefreshToken, OTPVerification, Permission, Role, UserPermission, UserRole, RolePermission, File, Notification, NotificationPreference. Multi-tenancy domainID pattern and soft-delete pattern defined.
Database setup flow and migration workflow. Init migration creates all core tables. Migration commands: prisma migrate dev (development), prisma migrate deploy (production).
User story definition: Database migration system for PostgreSQL with version control. Includes init migration, package.json scripts, MongoDB skip logic, README documentation.
Prisma ORM v6.16.0+ configuration, migration patterns, connection pooling, multi-tenancy support via domainID fields.
Migration rollback strategy, transactional migrations (PostgreSQL DDL support), shadow database validation, migration lock mechanism.
Prisma v6.16.0+ dependency, PostgreSQL 15+ database server, migration system integration points with PrismaService.

artifact.reason: Existing PrismaService extends PrismaClient, provides database connection lifecycle management. Migration will use this service for database operations.
Global module providing PrismaService. Migration system integrates with this module for database access.

Complete database schema for PostgreSQL. Init migration will generate SQL from this schema to create all tables: users, permissions, roles, refresh_tokens, otp_verifications, user_permissions, user_roles, role_permissions, files, notifications, notification_preferences.
Source schema file for PostgreSQL database. Contains original entity definitions copied to schema.prisma during setup.
Source schema file for MongoDB database. Used for MongoDB projects (schemaless, no migrations).
Existing scripts section. Will add migration commands: prisma:generate, prisma:migrate, prisma:deploy.
Setup script from Story 1.3 that copies schema files. Determines database type (PostgreSQL vs MongoDB) for migration logic.
Git ignore file. Must ensure prisma/migrations/ is NOT ignored (migrations need version control).

node.package: Prisma CLI for migrations and schema management

story-context.constraints:
**Multi-tenancy Pattern:** - Every entity MUST include domainID field (UUID type) - All queries MUST filter by domainID for data isolation - Migration SQL must include domainID columns and indexes
**Soft-Delete Pattern:** - User and File entities use deletedAt field (nullable DateTime) - Queries filter deletedAt IS NULL to exclude soft-deleted records - Migration creates deletedAt columns with proper indexes
**Foreign Key Constraints:** - Cascade deletes: RefreshToken, OTPVerification, UserPermission, UserRole (onDelete: Cascade) - User-related tables cascade on user deletion - Migration SQL must include CASCADE constraints
**Index Strategy:** - Index on domainID for all tables (multi-tenancy performance) - Index on frequently queried fields: email, token, userID, expiresAt - Unique constraints: [module, action], [domainID, name], [userID, permissionID, domainID] - Migration generates all indexes
**Migration Safety:** - Transactional DDL (PostgreSQL support - rollback on error) - Shadow database validation (Prisma creates temp DB to validate) - Migration lock file prevents concurrent migrations - All migrations committed to Git (NOT in .gitignore)
**Provider-Specific Logic:** - PostgreSQL: Full migration system (SQL files generated) - MongoDB: Skip migrations (schemaless database, no SQL) - Conditional logic based on schema.prisma provider field
**Version Control:** - migrations/ folder MUST NOT be in .gitignore - All migration files tracked in Git - Migration history maintained in _prisma_migrations table
story-context.interfaces:
**Prisma Migration CLI:** - Command: `npx prisma migrate dev --name ` - Creates new migration with timestamp prefix - Applies migration to database - Regenerates Prisma Client - Interactive (prompts for migration name)
- Command: `npx prisma migrate deploy` - Applies pending migrations only - Non-interactive (CI/CD safe) - Fails if schema drift detected
- Command: `npx prisma generate` - Generates Prisma Client from schema - Required after schema changes - Updates TypeScript types
- Command: `npx prisma migrate resolve --rolled-back ` - Marks failed migration as rolled back - Used for manual rollback resolution
**package.json Scripts to Add:** - `"prisma:generate": "prisma generate"` - Generate Prisma Client - `"prisma:migrate": "prisma migrate dev"` - Run migration in development - `"prisma:deploy": "prisma migrate deploy"` - Deploy migrations to production
**Migration File Structure:** ``` prisma/migrations/ ├── _init/ │ └── migration.sql (CREATE TABLE statements) ├── migration_lock.toml (provider: "postgresql") └── _prisma_migrations (metadata table, auto-created) ```
**PrismaService Interface (from Story 1.4):** - `PrismaService extends PrismaClient` - `async onModuleInit()` - Database connection - `async $queryRaw` - Raw SQL queries - Used for testing migration results

tests.standards: **Testing Framework:** Jest v30.0.0 (configured in package.json) **Test Location:** src/**/*.spec.ts for unit tests, test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts for E2E tests **Integration Testing:** Test migration execution, verify table creation, check schema constraints **Database:** Use test database for integration tests (separate from development DB) **Cleanup:** Drop tables between tests for clean state
tests.locations: - Unit tests: src/**/*.spec.ts - Integration tests: test/**/*.integration.spec.ts - E2E tests: test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts - Coverage: coverage/ directory (generated by jest --coverage)
tests.ideas:
**AC-1.5.1 Test Ideas:** - Verify prisma/migrations/ folder exists after migration - Check .gitignore does NOT contain prisma/migrations/ - Verify migration_lock.toml created with provider: "postgresql"
**AC-1.5.2 Test Ideas:** - Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` - Verify migration SQL file created in migrations/_init/ - Check SQL contains CREATE TABLE for all core entities - Verify _prisma_migrations table has init migration record - Query information_schema.tables to list all created tables
**AC-1.5.3 Test Ideas:** - Check package.json has prisma:generate script - Check package.json has prisma:migrate script - Check package.json has prisma:deploy script - Run `npm run prisma:generate` successfully - Run `npm run prisma:migrate` successfully
**AC-1.5.4 Test Ideas:** - Connect to PostgreSQL database after migration - Query each table to verify it exists - Check foreign key constraints (INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLE_CONSTRAINTS) - Verify indexes created (pg_indexes view) - Test simple INSERT into User table
**AC-1.5.5 Test Ideas:** - Test with MongoDB schema (schema.prisma provider: "mongodb") - Verify migration command gracefully skips or warns - Ensure no error thrown for MongoDB projects
**AC-1.5.6 Test Ideas:** - Verify README.md contains "Database Migrations" section - Check documentation includes migration workflow steps - Verify migration naming best practices documented
